commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 70s Skill Workshop lifecycle approval timeout and extends the embedded-mode regression test to assert 70s request and 80s gateway timeouts.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +4. Total +8 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main routes pending skill_workshop lifecycle actions through a 120s plugin approval wait while the Codex bridge defaults dynamic tool calls to 90s. I did not run the full live Gateway + Codex reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A bounded repair can move the timeout bound out of the global Skill Workshop approval policy and into the Codex dynamic-tool timeout path, then update the existing focused regression tests.

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): bound lifecycle approval timeout below Codex dynamic-tool watchdog
target.body:
### Summary

- `skill_workshop` lifecycle tool calls (apply/reject/quarantine) go through the Codex dynamic-tool bridge, which wraps every call with a 90s watchdog (`CODEX_DYNAMIC_TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000`, `dynamic-tool-execution.ts:23`)
- `resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval` returned a `requireApproval` object with no `timeoutMs`, so `resolvePluginToolApprovalTimeoutMs` fell back to `DEFAULT_PLUGIN_APPROVAL_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000` (`plugin-approvals.ts:50`)
- 90s watchdog < 120s approval wait → the watchdog fired first with a generic "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms" message; the approval prompt was left pending and the proposal was never applied
- Fix: add `timeoutMs: 70_000` to the `requireApproval` return in `policy.ts`; the approval now expires at 70s with a clean denial before the 90s watchdog, in all caller contexts
- `resolvePluginToolApprovalGatewayTimeoutMs` adds a 10s grace → gateway call bounded at 80s, well under the 90s watchdog

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `skill_workshop(action="apply")` from an agent session always timed out with "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop"; approval prompt never visible; proposal stayed pending

**Real environment tested:** source inspection + embedded-mode test harness with fake timers and mocked gateway calls

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix:** 11/11 tests pass; new assertions confirm `request.timeoutMs = 70_000` and gateway `timeoutParams.timeoutMs = 80_000` for both the direct apply path and the hook-adjusted path

**Observed result after fix:** approval timeout (70s) resolves before the Codex watchdog (90s); a timed-out approval produces a clean "denied" result; `approvalPolicy: auto` workaround no longer needed

**What was not tested:** live Gateway + Codex agent session; ClawSweeper noted this is a source-proven path and a fake-timer regression test covers the entry point

### Reproduction

1. Configure Skill Workshop with default `approvalPolicy: pending`
2. Start a Codex agent session
3. Prompt the agent to call `skill_workshop(action="apply", proposal_id="...")`
4. **Before this PR:** gateway returns "OpenClaw dynamic tool call timed out after 90000ms while running tool skill_workshop" at the 90s mark; proposal untouched
5. **After this PR:** approval prompt appears within the session; if unanswered after 70s, the call returns a clean "denied" result

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk:** reduces approval window from 120s to 70s for all skill_workshop lifecycle callers, not just Codex — operators who relied on the full 120s window lose 50s
- **Mitigation:** 70s is generous for an interactive approval; the `timeoutBehavior` default is `"deny"` so a timeout is safe; the old 120s window was only reachable via the `approvalPolicy: auto` workaround (which bypasses approval entirely), so no operator was actually using the 120s window in a non-Codex context for lifecycle actions

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Core
- [x] Agent runtime
- [x] Skills / Skill Workshop

### Linked Issue / PR

Fixes #91266
target.author: openperf

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
